en: Now, sometimes, I don't know if I feel well. That doesn't make any sense at all, either.
ko: 가끔은 몸 상태가 좋은지 잘 모르겠어요. 말이 안 되죠.
ja: Now, sometimes, 分かりません if I feel well. That doesn't make any sense at all, either.
zhTW: Now, sometimes, 我不知道 if I feel well. That doesn't make any sense at all, either.
vi: 가끔은 몸 상태가 좋은지 잘 모르겠어요. 말이 안 되죠.

en: What do you mean, sometimes you don't know whether you feel well?
ko: 몸 상태를 잘 모른다는 게 무슨 뜻이에요?

en: Because I've been in my body for years. Here he goes again.
ko: 슬픈 얘기야. 슬픈 건 아는데, 다음 문장을 모르겠어.

en: Go on, yeah. Here he goes again. The two minds.
ko: 가자, 그래. 또 시작이네. 두 개의 마음.

en: Now, if there was some sort of kit that the doctor said, how are you feeling, Mr Pilkington?
ko: 사람들은 항상 생각하잖아. 거기서 끝이야. 더 안 해도 생긴 거 알잖아.

en: And I go, oh, I think I feel all right. And they go, well, do you? Yeah, I don't know.
ko: 좋은 별명이야. 번 좋아했어? 이런.
ja: And I go, oh, 思うに I feel all right. And they go, well, do you? Yeah, 分かりません.
zhTW: And I go, oh, 我覺得 I feel all right. And they go, well, do you? Yeah, 我不知道.

en: And they go, well, step into the machine, right? Get into the machine.
ko: 이런 키트가 있어서 의사가 "기분이 어떠세요, 필킹턴 씨?"라고 물으면,

en: And if he could somehow transfer my feeling into his body
ko: "괜찮은 것 같아요"라고 하면 "정말요?" "글쎄요, 모르겠어요."

en: so I could feel how he feels, and then he can feel how I feel, and he'll go, oh, you're not well at all.
ko: 그러면 "기계에 들어가세요"라고 하겠죠. 기계에 들어가는 거예요.

en: Your heartbeat's irregular, for a start. You are a mental case.
ko: 그가 제 느낌을 자기 몸으로 옮길 수 있다면

en: I don't know what that is. I don't know what this is. But why can't the doctor make that observation like a doctor does?
ko: 제가 그의 느낌을 느끼고, 그가 제 느낌을 느끼면, "전혀 안 좋으시네요."
ja: 分かりません what that is. 分かりません what this is. But why can't the doctor make that observation like a doctor does?
zhTW: 我不知道 what that is. 我不知道 what this is. But why can't the doctor make that observation like a doctor does?
vi: 제가 그의 느낌을 느끼고, 그가 제 느낌을 느끼면, "전혀 안 좋으시네요."

en: Why can't the doctor, using his knowledge, observe the person with the swell? Right. I tell you what.
ko: 가라, 필킹턴.

en: This is like a scene from Ghost. This is like Patrick Swayze.
ko: 택시 앞에 앉아 있었는데 아빠가 가서 태우자고 했어. 이름도 못 말해. 악마야.

en: Listen, it's hard to explain an ache. So he'd have to feel the ache, and then he could make an assumption from it.
ko: 이건 고스트 영화의 한 장면 같아요. 패트릭 스웨이지 같아요.

en: Thank you. So he would presumably go through training going in the machine, and they'd bring in various sick people,
ko: 통증을 설명하기 어렵잖아요. 의사가 통증을 느끼면 판단할 수 있을 거예요.
ja: ありがとう. So he would presumably go through training going in the machine, and they'd bring in various sick people,
zhTW: 謝謝. So he would presumably go through training going in the machine, and they'd bring in various sick people,
vi: 통증을 설명하기 어렵잖아요. 의사가 통증을 느끼면 판단할 수 있을 거예요.

en: and he would experience and feel every single ailment. Hey, listen.
ko: 그리고 그는 모든 고통을 경험하고 느낄 거야. 이봐, 들어봐.

en: Let's not dismiss this idea out of hand, of course, because you'll famously remember, Rick,
ko: 상관없어. 뭐라고 불렀어? 스티브. 아니. 칼, 솔직히 인상이 이미 안 좋아.

en: that Carl had the idea of a man who can grow backwards.
ko: 연수 중에 기계에 들어가서 여러 환자를 체험하면,

en: So he's born as an old man, dead when he dies as a young baby.
ko: 모든 질병을 직접 경험하고 느끼게 되는 거죠. 잠깐.

en: I think we're all looking forward to the new Brad Pitt film.
ko: 뭐라고 했는지 말해. 아빠한테 뭐가 올까 봐 무서운 게 아니라 그 사람이...
ja: 思うに we're all looking forward to the new Brad Pitt film.
zhTW: 我覺得 we're all looking forward to the new Brad Pitt film.
vi: 뭐라고 했는지 말해. 아빠한테 뭐가 올까 봐 무서운 게 아니라 그 사람이...

en: That is exactly that plot. Isn't it a bit strange?
ko: 이 아이디어를 바로 무시하지 맙시다. 칼이 거꾸로 자라는 사람 아이디어를 냈잖아요.

en: Carl said the most exciting words.
ko: 거꾸로 자랄 수 있는 사람 아이디어요.

en: He said, I've had another film idea. Wow.
ko: 노인으로 태어나서 아기로 죽는 거죠.

en: Sell it to me. I'm a Hollywood executive. Sell me the film.
ko: 새 브래드 피트 영화가 기대되네요.

en: So what I was thinking is it starts off,
ko: 바로 그 줄거리잖아요. 좀 이상하지 않아요?

en: and the people, you know, you're seeing into their lives from, like, the morning.
ko: 칼이 가장 흥미로운 말을 했어요.

en: So it's like a nice sunny day. Radio's on.
ko: 또 영화 아이디어가 있다고요. 와.

en: You know, they're going about the day. They say, oh, what are we doing tonight?
ko: 그들은 하루를 보내고 있어. 오늘 밤 뭐 할까? 라고 말해.
ja: ほら, they're going about the day. They say, oh, what are we doing tonight?
zhTW: 你知道, they're going about the day. They say, oh, what are we doing tonight?
vi: 그들은 하루를 보내고 있어. 오늘 밤 뭐 할까? 라고 말해.

en: And you're thinking, oh, they've got a nice life. He walks out the house, gets hit by a car.
ko: 제가 생각한 건 이렇게 시작돼요.

en: Oh. So Clive Warren's dead.
ko: 사람들의 삶을 아침부터 들여다보는 거예요.

en: Now, what happens is she's devastated.
ko: 좋은 날씨에 라디오가 켜져 있고.

en: Doctor says, your husband's dead.
ko: 생긴 걸 대충 예상했고 아빠가 차에 있을래? 데려다줄게, 안 되니까. 스포어 데드, 예쁜 눈을 가졌어.

en: She's like, oh, God. What happens then is he says, but listen,
ko: 그녀는, 아, 신이여. 그때 그는 이렇게 말해, 하지만 들어봐,

en: what we can do now, we can take the brain out.
ko: 아빠가 택시 앞에 앉아 있었는데 가서 태우자고 했어. 뭐라고 불렀는지 말 못 해.

en: Right, right. The brain, it can run on half of it.
ko: 세상에. 이거 다 지어낸 거잖아요.

en: You've actually got a full brain. So this was in my mind still.
ko: 실화가 아니라는 거지.

en: Well, half your mind, yeah. So I said, what happens is Morgan Freeman says,
ko: 네 마음의 반이지. 그래서 모건 프리먼이 이렇게 말해요.

en: been working on this, you can run your life on half a brain.
ko: 이걸 연구해왔는데, 뇌 반쪽으로도 살 수 있다고.

en: She's sort of a bit like, what are you telling me this for now?
ko: 그녀가 좀 당황해서, 지금 왜 이걸 말하는 거예요?

en: My husband's just died, like, 20 minutes ago.
ko: 남편이 20분 전에 막 죽었는데.

en: And he goes, yeah, but if we're going to do this, we've got to act quick.
ko: 그가 말해요, 그래도 이걸 하려면 빨리 해야 해요.

en: She's like, do what? He says, look, you know, what are we going to do here?
ko: 듣고 있지 않겠지? 헤드폰이 안 맞을 텐데. 뭐라고 불렀는지 말해.

en: We can either turn the switch off, or we can put his head in your head. But why would you?
ko: 우리는 스위치를 끌 수도 있고, 아니면 그의 머리를 네 머리 속에 넣을 수도 있어. 하지만 왜 그렇게 해야 하지?

en: So what he does, so what they do then, they're going to take half his brain.
ko: 그래서 뇌의 절반을 꺼내는 거예요.

en: Half of his brain, take out half of hers, pop it in place. Why would she do that?
ko: 그의 뇌 반쪽을 꺼내고, 그녀의 반쪽을 빼서 넣는 거죠. 왜 그렇게 하겠어요?

en: Because she loves him. But hold on, wait, wait, wait, wait.
ko: 말해. 상관없어. 말해. 스티브. 그만둘 거야. 알고 싶어.

en: What would she then be? Because this is what I'm trying to tell you. Okay, okay, sorry.
ko: 그러면 그녀는 뭐가 되는 거죠? 이게 말하려는 거예요. 알겠어요, 미안.

en: So what happens is he's gone, but you'll have his thoughts.
ko: 그가 떠나지만, 그의 생각이 남는 거예요.

en: So in the morning, when you say, oh, I don't know what to have.
ko: 아침에 뭘 먹을까 고민할 때,
ja: So in the morning, when you say, oh, 分かりません what to have.
zhTW: So in the morning, when you say, oh, 我不知道 what to have.

en: Well, they have cornflakes. Is better the brain will sort of say. Have a Weetabix.
ko: 콘플레이크가 있네. 뇌가 위타빅스 먹으라고 할 거예요.

en: Have shredded wheat or whatever. And she's like, oh yeah, good idea.
ko: 9살쯤. 좋았어? 감자에 드레스 입힌 걸 기대했어? 좋은 별명이야.

en: Sorry, sorry, so the point of this film is that the dead man can remind her
ko: 예상은 했고 아빠가 차에 있을래? 데려다줄게, 안 되니까. 스포어 데드, 예쁜 눈을 가졌어.

en: what breakfast cereal she likes. Yeah.
ko: 그녀가 어떤 아침 시리얼을 좋아하는지. 그래.

en: But anyway, this is where you get them.
ko: 어쨌든, 여기서 관객을 잡는 거야.

en: It's the most ludicrous idea for a film I've ever heard.
ko: 내가 들어본 영화 아이디어 중 가장 말도 안 되는 거야.

en: It's the maddest. It's honestly, it really is the ramblings of a mental case.
ko: 가장 미친 거야. 정신이상자의 헛소리야.

en: I have to say though, I am hooked now. I want to know what's going to happen next in the story.
ko: 솔직히 지금 완전 빠져들었어요. 다음에 뭐가 일어나는지 알고 싶어요.

en: Oh Christ. Remember, I was making all this up.
ko: 세상에. 이거 다 지어낸 거잖아요.

en: It's not based on a true story then.
ko: 실화가 아니라는 거지.
